Performing initial audit of PKGBUILD to ensure that we can safely run `makepkg`
LLM auditresponse for PKGBUILD:
<security_assessment>
<decision>SAFE</decision>
<details>
The PKGBUILD defines sources for both x86_64 and aarch64 architectures, pointing to official GitHub release binaries under the ruffle-rs/ruffle repository. The URLs are static and use HTTPS, targeting a legitimate and expected upstream source. The `sha512sums` are provided and not set to 'SKIP', indicating checksum verification is performed for integrity. There is no `pkgver()` function defined, so no dynamic version resolution occurs during sourcing. The global scope contains only standard metadata and variable assignments, with no command substitutions, function calls, or external code execution. The potentially sensitive operations (like `cd`, `install`) are confined within the `package()` function, which will not be executed due to the `--noprepare` and `--nobuild` flags. Thus, sourcing this PKGBUILD and downloading the sources poses no risk of arbitrary code execution or data exfiltration.
</details>
<summary>
No dangerous code executes during source download
</summary>
</security_assessment>

+ Initial PKGBUILD audit complete [grey50]-- No dangerous code executes during source download[/]
Running makepkg --nobuild --nodeps --noprepare to download sources for agent...
makepkg --nobuild --nodeps --noprepare successful
Running makepkg --printsrcinfo to get source files for agent...
makepkg --printsrcinfo successful
Reviewing required files...
[0/1] Reviewing PKGBUILD...
LLM auditresponse for PKGBUILD:
<security_assessment>
<decision>SAFE</decision>
<details>
The PKGBUILD fetches a pre-built binary release of Ruffle from the official GitHub repository (github.com/ruffle-rs/ruffle) over HTTPS, targeting specific architecture-dependent tarballs for x86_64 and aarch64. The source URLs are hardcoded to a specific release date (nightly-2026-06-19), and corresponding SHA-512 checksums are provided, ensuring integrity verification. No dynamic or untrusted downloads, obfuscated code, or runtime modifications are present.

The `package()` function performs standard file installations into appropriate system directories: binaries to `/usr/bin`, documentation and licenses to `/usr/share`, and desktop integration files (icons, .desktop, and AppStream metadata) to their respective locations. All operations are confined to the package build directory and target `$pkgdir`, with no attempts to access external network resources, exfiltrate data, or modify system state beyond what is expected for desktop application installation.

This PKGBUILD follows standard AUR packaging practices for a `-bin` package: it uses verified upstream binaries, includes proper metadata, and installs only the necessary files without side effects. There are no dangerous commands (e.g., `eval`, `curl|bash`), no use of `git pull` or similar runtime content injection, and no deviation from safe, reproducible packaging norms.
</details>
<summary>
Standard binary package with verified sources
</summary>
</security_assessment>

LLM auditresponse for rs.ruffle.Ruffle.desktop:
<security_assessment>
<decision>SAFE</decision>
<details>
The provided file is a standard desktop entry (`.desktop` file) for the Ruffle application, used to integrate it into desktop environments on Linux systems. It declares standard fields such as name, comment, icon, executable (`Exec=ruffle %u`), supported MIME types, categories, and keywords in multiple languages. The file contains no executable code, scripts, or embedded commands. It does not perform any network operations, file manipulations, or system modifications. The use of `ruffle %u` in the Exec field is standard practice for launching applications with optional URL arguments. All content aligns with typical desktop file specifications defined by freedesktop.org.
</details>
<summary>
Standard desktop entry, no executable code
</summary>
</security_assessment>

LLM auditresponse for README.md:
<security_assessment>
<decision>SAFE</decision>
<details>
The provided file is a README.md from the Ruffle project's AUR package repository. It contains documentation and instructions for building and using Ruffle, an open-source Flash emulator. The content consists solely of markdown (with HTML entities) describing the project, its build prerequisites, usage instructions, structure, sponsors, and licensing information.

There is no executable code, scripts, or package build instructions (such as a PKGBUILD) in this file. All links point to official and relevant project resources (GitHub, Discord, sponsors, downloads, etc.). The file does not contain obfuscated content, suspicious commands, network exfiltration attempts, or any behavior indicative of a supply-chain attack.

As this is a standard informational README commonly found in AUR packages and contains no malicious content, the file is safe.
</details>
<summary>
Documentation-only file, no executable code.
</summary>
</security_assessment>

[2/4] Reviewing src/LICENSE.md, src/extras/rs.ruffle.Ruffle.metainfo.xml...

LLM auditresponse for LICENSE.md:
<security_assessment>
<decision>SAFE</decision>
<details>
The provided file is LICENSE.md for the Ruffle project, which contains licensing information for the Ruffle application and its third-party dependencies. It outlines the dual licensing under Apache License 2.0 and MIT License, along with the licenses of various dependent libraries.

The content consists entirely of license texts, copyright notices, and attributions. There are no executable commands, network operations, file manipulations, obfuscated code, or any other elements that could indicate malicious behavior. It is a standard documentation file used to comply with open source license requirements.

All information within this file pertains to legal terms and attributions, with no indication of data exfiltration, backdoors, or supply chain attacks. The file serves a legitimate and expected purpose in open source software distribution.
</details>
<summary>
Documentation file containing only license and attribution information
</summary>
</security_assessment>

[3/4] Reviewing src/extras/rs.ruffle.Ruffle.metainfo.xml...
+ Reviewed src/LICENSE.md. Status: SAFE -- Documentation file containing only license and attribution information
LLM auditresponse for rs.ruffle.Ruffle.metainfo.xml:
<security_assessment>
<decision>SAFE</decision>
<details>
The provided file is a standard AppStream metadata file (`rs.ruffle.Ruffle.metainfo.xml`) used for describing desktop applications in Linux distributions. It contains information such as the application name, description, developer details, project URLs, release history, supported media types, and screenshots. All URLs point to legitimate and expected locations related to the Ruffle project, such as its official website (https://ruffle.rs), GitHub repository (https://github.com/ruffle-rs/ruffle), donation page (https://opencollective.com/ruffle), and translation platform (https://crowdin.com/project/ruffle).

There are no executable commands, scripts, or network calls embedded within this XML file. The content is purely descriptive and follows the AppStream specification. No obfuscation, suspicious payloads, or unauthorized data exfiltration mechanisms are present. All fields are consistent with standard packaging practices for an open-source emulator project aiming to preserve Flash content.

The file poses no security risk and is entirely safe.
</details>
<summary>
Standard metadata file with no executable code or malicious content
</summary>
</security_assessment>
